data.task:
Ticket 0045 — Atom 1 of N: self-healing reconcile of the tickets queue (acceptance item 1, the PRIMARY/durable fix). Keep `closeTicket` the SOLE owner of `order.json` pruning — do NOT add a second pruning call in a selection caller.

Problem: when a ticket-launched run finishes for a ticket whose file is already in `closed/` (it was closed off-spine, so its id was never pruned), `closeTicket()` returns `{ closed: false, reason: 'already-closed' }` and the daemon logs `ticket-close-skipped` WITHOUT reconciling the stale `order.json` entry. The queue stays dirty and the closed ticket relaunches on every subsequent selection.

Change (one owner): 
1. In `packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts`: on the `already-closed` path (the early return at close.ts:81-83 when there is no open file but the ticket exists as closed), still call `pruneTicketOrder` idempotently and surface the pruned file to the caller so it can be committed. Extend the `closed:false` result variant to carry the pruned `files` (e.g. `{ closed: false, reason: 'already-closed', files: readonly string[] }`, paths relative to `repoPath`, same shape as the `closed:true` files). Leave `missing-open-ticket` unchanged (no file => prune that stale id too if present — an id with no open AND no closed file is still stale and should be pruned; mirror the same `files` surfacing for that branch). `pruneTicketOrder` already returns null when the id is absent, so this stays a no-op under normal operation.
2. In `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` `closeTicketAfterSuccessfulRun` (~line 438): on `!close.closed`, if `close.files` is non-empty, commit them via `commitGovernance` with a message like `governance: reconcile stale ticket ${ticketId} order entry via run ${result.runId}` and append an audit action (e.g. `ticket-order-reconciled`) instead of only `ticket-close-skipped`. When there are no files, keep the existing `ticket-close-skipped` audit.

Tests (pin the behavior, do not weaken existing pins): add a test that launching/finishing a run for a ticket whose file is already in `closed/` but whose id is STILL in `order.json` leaves `order.json` PRUNED afterward. Put a core-level test in `packages/core/tests/tickets.test.ts` (call `closeTicket` directly: closed file present + id in order.json => returns `closed:false, reason:'already-closed'`, files includes order.json, and order.json on disk is pruned) AND a daemon-level test in `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts` that `closeTicketAfterSuccessfulRun`/the run-completion path commits the reconcile. Keep the existing pins at `packages/core/tests/tickets.test.ts:214` and `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts:855` green.

Must not break: normal close path (open ticket => closed + pruned + Resolution stamped) unchanged; idempotent (running twice is a no-op, no spurious commit when order.json has no stale id). 

Exit criterion: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon test` passes (or the repo's scoped test command for these two packages), plus typecheck. Report the exact commands, exit codes, and the new/changed test names. Write-scope: packages/core/src/tickets/**, packages/core/tests/tickets.test.ts, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts.

data.task:
Ticket 0045 — Atom 2 of N: the belt-and-suspenders governance guard (acceptance item 2). Add the tickets-side analog of `findOrphanedPriorities`, for the INVERSE failure: an id present in `cocoder/tickets/order.json` that is NOT an open ticket (it is closed, or missing entirely). Atom 1 already fixed the self-healing reconcile; this atom only adds the detector + tests so a stale queue head can never silently persist.

Implement (one owner — put it next to the existing ticket-order helpers):
1. In `packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts`, add and export `findStaleTicketOrderEntries(ticketsDir: string): Promise<string[]>`. It reads `order.json` via the existing `readManifest` helper and the ticket set via `readTicketFiles` (already imported as `readTicketFiles`), and returns the sorted list of manifest ids that are NOT the id of an OPEN ticket (i.e. closed ids and ids with no ticket file at all). Reuse the existing open-ticket view — `tickets.filter((t) => t.state === 'open')` — so this stays the same open-only definition `writeTicketOrder` already uses (priority-order.ts:155-158); do not invent a second notion of 'open'. Return `[]` when there is no manifest. Mirror the shape/style of `findOrphanedPriorities`/`liveUnlistedPriorityIds` (sorted, deduped).

Tests in `packages/daemon/tests/priority-order.test.ts` (mirror the existing priority guard tests):
2. A synthetic unit test: build a temp tickets dir with `open/0003-*.md` (status: Open) and `closed/0004-*.md` (status: Closed), write `order.json = ['0003','0004','0009']` (0009 has no file). Assert `findStaleTicketOrderEntries` returns `['0004','0009']` (closed + missing), and that after pruning order.json to `['0003']` it returns `[]`. Use the same temp-dir + frontmatter style already in this file / tickets.test.ts.
3. The LIVE-repo governance guard, mirroring the existing `'live priorities all resolve through order.json...'` test (priority-order.test.ts:42-47): resolve `repoRoot` the same way (`join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '..', '..', '..')`) and assert `findStaleTicketOrderEntries(join(repoRoot, 'cocoder', 'tickets'))` returns `[]`. This must be GREEN now (the live order.json was hand-cleaned in bbe6943); if it is not green, STOP and report the offending ids rather than editing order.json to force green.

Must not break: no runtime selection/launch wiring changes in this atom (that optional refactor is out of scope here); `findOrphanedPriorities` and all existing priority-order tests stay untouched and green. Do not weaken any existing pin.

Exit criterion: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon test` passes (including the new live-repo guard) and `--filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` is clean. Report exact commands, exit codes, the new test names, and confirm the live-repo guard passed against the real cocoder/tickets/order.json. Write-scope: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts, packages/daemon/tests/priority-order.test.ts.

data.task:
Ticket 0045 — Atom 3 of N: off-spine close PREVENTION in the Deb repair lane (acceptance item 3). This is base-orchestration governance: it lands in the base Deb persona and is pinned by the base persona test. Both files are under packages/** (your write scope).

Background (the incident this prevents): a Deb-repair commit (2796bb5) fixed ticket 0044's code AND hand-closed the ticket in the same commit — it set `status: Closed`, moved the file into `cocoder/tickets/closed/`, and edited `INDEX.md`, but NEVER called the `closeTicket()` spine. Result: no `## Resolution` stamp and, critically, `cocoder/tickets/order.json` was never pruned, so the closed ticket relaunched. Atoms 1 (self-heal reconcile) and 2 (stale-order guard) catch the residue; this atom stops Deb from creating it.

Change 1 — `packages/personas/base/deb.md`: add a clear governance instruction to Deb's repair discipline (place it near the existing repair/commit-spine guidance, e.g. the 'Make orchestration repairs stick' / repair-dialogue section). It must state, in Deb's voice and matching the file's existing CoCoder-flavored style: when a repair fixes the underlying issue of a tracked ticket, NEVER hand-close the ticket inside the repair commit — i.e. never move its file into `cocoder/tickets/closed/`, never rewrite its `status:` to Closed, and never hand-edit the tickets `INDEX.md`/`order.json` to mark it closed. Instead, either (a) close it through the governed close spine `closeTicket()` (the SOLE owner that prunes `cocoder/tickets/order.json` AND stamps the `## Resolution` section), or (b) leave the ticket OPEN so the run-success close path closes it through that same spine. Hand-closing is forbidden precisely because it bypasses `order.json` pruning and leaves a stale queue head that gets relaunched. Keep it tight (a few sentences / one bullet), consistent with the surrounding bullets; do not restate unrelated rules.

Change 2 — `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts`: add a `test(...)` inside the existing `describe('basePersonasDir', ...)` block that reads `deb.md` and pins the new rule, mirroring the existing deb.md pin style (read file, normalize whitespace if needed, `.toContain(...)`). Assert the presence of the load-bearing phrases — at minimum: that Deb must route ticket closure through `closeTicket()` (or the governed close spine) rather than hand-moving the file, that hand-closing / hand-editing `order.json` in a repair commit is forbidden, and that the alternative is to leave the ticket open for the run-success close path. Pick exact substrings that appear verbatim in your Change-1 text so the test is a true pin. Do NOT weaken or alter any existing assertion in this file.

Must not break: every existing base-personas test stays green (including the ones pinning Deb's scope and repair-dialogue language at base-personas.test.ts:64-86); no other persona file changes; no runtime/daemon code changes in this atom.

Exit criterion: the personas package test suite passes (e.g. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/personas test`) including your new test, and that package's typecheck is clean. Report the exact command(s), exit code(s), the new test name, and the exact substrings you pinned. Write-scope: packages/personas/base/deb.md, packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts.
